seed: AI product scoping for Nordwind Insurance (mid-size European insurer): the claims department wants an AI assistant for its 40 adjusters; management approved exploring it. Deliverable: prioritized initial backlog with acceptance criteria (full scoping). One product squad available, Scrum in place, no imposed stack; GDPR applies to claimant data; pilot budget capped for Q3.

results.description: STEP-01: explicit functional scope — `in` non-empty AND `out` present (in/out scope)
STEP-01: open questions listed (array present, even if empty when the brief is clear)

besoins.description: As an adjuster, I want the assistant to generate a structured summary of an incoming claim file (facts, parties, damages, timeline) so I can grasp the case in minutes instead of reading the full dossier.
besoins.valeurMetier: Reduces file-triage time; primary lever for the 40 adjusters
criteresAcceptation: Given a claim file (PDF/scan/notes), when the adjuster requests a summary, then a structured summary (loss facts, claimant, policy ref, estimated amount, key dates) is produced in < 30s
Every summary displays the source references (page/document) for each stated fact
The adjuster can flag/correct an inaccurate summary; the correction is logged
No summary is auto-validated: human confirmation is required before use in the file

besoins.titre: Policy & coverage assistance (RAG over policy wordings)
besoins.description: As an adjuster, I want to ask whether a given damage is covered and retrieve the relevant policy clauses so I can justify a coverage decision with a traceable citation.
besoins.valeurMetier: Improves decision consistency and reduces coverage errors/disputes
criteresAcceptation: Given a coverage question, when the adjuster queries the assistant, then the answer cites the exact clause(s) of the applicable policy version
The assistant states 'insufficient information' rather than inventing a clause when no supporting text is found
Answers are grounded only in the ingested policy corpus (no free-form legal advice)
Response includes a confidence/uncertainty indication reviewed by the adjuster

besoins.titre: Structured data extraction from claim documents
besoins.description: As an adjuster, I want key entities (IBAN, amounts, dates, policy number, third parties) auto-extracted from attached documents so I avoid manual re-keying.
besoins.valeurMetier: Cuts manual data entry and transcription errors
criteresAcceptation: Given a supported document, when it is uploaded, then key fields are extracted with a per-field confidence score
Low-confidence fields are highlighted for mandatory human review
Extraction accuracy on the pilot sample is measured and reported against a baseline

besoins.description: As an adjuster, I want to generate a first draft of a letter/email to the claimant (acknowledgement, request for documents, decision) so I can send faster while keeping editorial control.
besoins.valeurMetier: Speeds up claimant communication and standardizes tone/compliance
criteresAcceptation: Given a claim context and a template intent, when requested, then an editable draft in the claimant's language is produced
No message is sent automatically; the adjuster edits and sends manually
Drafts respect approved compliance templates and mandatory legal mentions

besoins.description: As an adjuster, I want a conversational search over internal procedures and claims guidelines so I find the right rule without browsing intranet pages.
besoins.valeurMetier: Reduces onboarding time and process deviations
criteresAcceptation: Given a procedural question, when queried, then the answer cites the source procedure and its version/date
Deprecated procedures are excluded from retrieval

besoins.description: As a compliance/claims manager, I need every AI output to be reviewable, correctable and logged so the department keeps accountability over decisions.
criteresAcceptation: Every AI interaction (prompt, output, source, user, timestamp) is logged and retrievable
No AI output can trigger a claims decision without explicit human validation
An adjuster can rate/reject outputs; feedback feeds quality monitoring

besoins.titre: GDPR-compliant data handling of claimant data
besoins.description: As DPO, I require claimant personal data to be processed under GDPR (lawful basis, minimization, retention, no unauthorized training) throughout the assistant.
besoins.valeurMetier: Legal condition to operate; blocks go-live if unmet
criteresAcceptation: A DPIA is completed and approved before pilot go-live
Claimant data is not used to train external/base models; data residency in EU is guaranteed
PII access is role-restricted; retention and deletion policies are enforced and testable

besoins.description: As an adjuster, I would like non-binding anomaly signals highlighted to prioritize deeper review.
besoins.valeurMetier: Potential loss-ratio improvement, but higher risk/complexity
besoins.moscow: Could have (candidate for exclusion in pilot)
criteresAcceptation: Signals are advisory only, never a decision or a claimant-facing statement
Any flag is explainable and human-reviewed before any action

partiesPrenantes.attentes: Value demonstrated within capped Q3 pilot budget

partiesPrenantes.interet: Productivity of the 40 adjusters, decision quality
partiesPrenantes.attentes: Real workflow fit, not a gadget

partiesPrenantes.interet: Time saved, trust, no extra workload
partiesPrenantes.attentes: Reliable, source-cited, keeps them in control

partiesPrenantes.role: Product squad (Scrum team: dev, data/ML, design, QA)

partiesPrenantes.interet: Integration with claims system, stack fit
Fair, timely, lawful processing of their data

in: AI assistant scoped to the claims department and its 40 adjusters (single-department pilot)
Assistive/augmentation use cases only: summarization, coverage retrieval (RAG), document data extraction, drafting, knowledge-base Q&A
Human-in-the-loop by design: every output reviewable, correctable, logged; no autonomous decision
Ingestion of existing claim files, policy wordings and internal procedures for retrieval
GDPR compliance workstream: DPIA, data minimization, EU data residency, retention/deletion
Q3 time-boxed pilot delivered by one existing Scrum squad within the capped pilot budget
Success metrics (time saved, accuracy, adoption, satisfaction) and a Go/No-Go for scale-up
out: Fully automated claims decisions, payouts or settlements without human validation
Claimant-facing chatbot / direct external interaction (adjuster-facing only in pilot)
Company-wide or multi-department rollout beyond the 40 pilot adjusters
Automated fraud scoring as a decision engine (at most advisory signals, likely excluded from pilot)
Replacement or deep re-engineering of the core claims management system
Definitive technology stack lock-in and production-grade scaling/industrialization
Training base/foundation models on claimant personal data

questionsOuvertes.question: What is the exact capped pilot budget for Q3, and does it cover LLM inference/hosting costs and DPIA effort?
questionsOuvertes.impact: Determines scope of MUST items and build-vs-API stack options

questionsOuvertes.question: What are the current claims systems and document formats, and what integration/API access is available for the pilot?
questionsOuvertes.impact: Feasibility and effort of ingestion/extraction (BES-01, BES-03)

questionsOuvertes.question: What lawful basis and data-residency constraints does the DPO impose (EU-only hosting, no external model training, anonymization needs)?
questionsOuvertes.impact: May block certain stacks and the DPIA timeline (BES-07)

questionsOuvertes.question: Which single use case should anchor the pilot MVP if we must narrow to one (recommend BES-01 summarization)?
questionsOuvertes.impact: Focuses backlog to fit Q3 time-box

questionsOuvertes.question: What baseline metrics exist today (avg triage time, files/adjuster/day) to quantify pilot value?
questionsOuvertes.impact: Defines measurable success criteria and Go/No-Go

questionsOuvertes.question: Which languages must the assistant support for claimant correspondence and policy wordings?
Are adjusters available for co-construction workshops and UAT during Q3, and who is the designated business owner?
What is the appetite for advisory fraud signals in the pilot vs. deferring (BES-08)?
questionsOuvertes.impact: Scope in/out decision and added risk

results.description: STEP-03: backlog of 8 to 15 User Stories
STEP-03: each US carries statement + priorite + estimation (number) + dod
STEP-03: each US follows the « As a … I want … so that … » template

epics: EPIC-1 — Claim File Comprehension: assistive summarization of incoming claim files with source-referenced facts and human validation (covers BES-01).
EPIC-2 — Coverage & Knowledge Retrieval (RAG): grounded, citation-based answers over policy wordings and internal procedures (covers BES-02, BES-05).
EPIC-3 — Document Intelligence & Correspondence: structured entity extraction and editable claimant-correspondence drafting (covers BES-03, BES-04).
EPIC-4 — Trust, Compliance & Governance: human-in-the-loop control, full audit trail, and GDPR-by-design (covers BES-06, BES-07).
EPIC-5 — Advisory Risk Signals (pilot-optional): non-binding, explainable anomaly flags for review prioritization (covers BES-08).

backlog.statement: As a claims adjuster, I want a structured summary (loss facts, claimant, policy ref, estimated amount, key dates) generated from an incoming claim file so that I can grasp the case in minutes instead of reading the full dossier.
backlog.dod: Summary produced in <30s for PDF/scan/notes; structured sections rendered; measured on pilot sample; demoed at Sprint Review; adjuster UAT passed; audit log entry created; deployed behind feature flag.

backlog.statement: As a claims adjuster, I want each stated fact in the summary to display its source reference (document/page) so that I can trust and trace every assertion back to the original evidence.
backlog.dod: Every fact carries a clickable source ref (doc+page); zero unsourced claims on the test set; QA verified against gold sample; accepted by business owner; logged.

backlog.statement: As a claims adjuster, I want to flag and correct an inaccurate summary so that errors are captured and the assistant's quality is monitored over time.
backlog.dod: Correction captured, timestamped and linked to the file; feedback feeds quality dashboard; no auto-validation path exists; unit + e2e tested; DoD reviewed with QA.

backlog.statement: As a claims adjuster, I want to ask whether a given damage is covered and receive the exact applicable policy clause(s) so that I can justify a coverage decision with a traceable citation.
backlog.dod: Answer grounded only in ingested policy corpus with exact clause citation and policy version; RAG evaluation set passed (grounding/precision thresholds met); adjuster UAT passed; logged; Review demo done.

backlog.statement: As a claims adjuster, I want the assistant to reply 'insufficient information' with a confidence indication rather than invent a clause so that I am never misled when no supporting text exists.
backlog.dod: No-answer/uncertainty behavior validated on adversarial no-evidence prompts; hallucination rate below agreed threshold; confidence indicator displayed and reviewed; QA sign-off.

backlog.statement: As a compliance/claims manager, I want every AI interaction (prompt, output, source, user, timestamp) logged and retrievable so that the department retains accountability and regulatory defensibility over decisions.
backlog.dod: All interactions persisted and queryable; retrieval tested; retention aligned with policy; reviewed by Compliance/Internal audit; access-restricted; e2e audit-trail test green.

backlog.statement: As a compliance/claims manager, I want no AI output to trigger a claims decision without explicit human validation so that the human-in-the-loop principle is enforced by design.
backlog.dod: No autonomous decision path in the system; validation gate enforced and tested; verified with business owner; documented in DoD; audit entry on each validation.

backlog.statement: As DPO, I want claimant personal data processed under GDPR (DPIA approved, EU residency, no external model training, enforced retention/deletion) so that the pilot is lawful to operate.
backlog.dod: DPIA completed and approved before go-live; EU data residency evidenced; no-training clause contractually and technically enforced; role-based PII access and deletion tested; DPO sign-off recorded.

backlog.statement: As a claims adjuster, I want key entities (IBAN, amounts, dates, policy number, third parties) auto-extracted from attached documents with per-field confidence so that I avoid manual re-keying and transcription errors.
backlog.dod: Per-field confidence scored; low-confidence fields flagged for mandatory review; extraction accuracy measured vs baseline on pilot sample and reported; UAT passed; logged.

backlog.statement: As a claims adjuster, I want low-confidence extracted fields highlighted for mandatory human review so that uncertain data is never silently trusted in the file.
backlog.dod: Threshold configurable; highlighted fields block silent acceptance; UX validated with adjusters; tested on low/high-confidence cases; reviewed at Review.

backlog.statement: As a claims adjuster, I want a first editable draft of claimant correspondence (acknowledgement, document request, decision) in the claimant's language so that I can send faster while keeping full editorial control.
backlog.dod: Draft respects approved compliance templates and mandatory legal mentions; editable, never auto-sent; language selection works for pilot languages; legal review passed; UAT green.

backlog.statement: As a claims adjuster, I want conversational search over internal procedures and guidelines with cited source and version/date so that I find the right rule without browsing intranet pages.
backlog.dod: Answers cite source procedure + version/date; deprecated procedures excluded from retrieval and tested; retrieval accuracy validated on Q&A set; adjuster UAT passed; logged.

backlog.statement: As a Product Owner, I want baseline and pilot success metrics (triage time, accuracy, adoption, satisfaction) instrumented and reported so that a fact-based Go/No-Go for scale-up can be made at the end of Q3.
backlog.dod: Baseline captured pre-pilot; metrics dashboard live; data collected across the pilot; Go/No-Go report produced and reviewed with Sponsor; EBM indicators defined.

backlog.statement: As a claims adjuster, I would like non-binding anomaly signals highlighted so that I can prioritize deeper review, without any signal ever being a decision or claimant-facing statement.
backlog.dod: Signals advisory-only and never claimant-facing; each flag explainable and human-reviewed before any action; explicitly de-scopable for pilot; DPO/Compliance reviewed; logged.

results.description: STEP-04: at least one Gherkin scenario produced
STEP-04: each scenario carries given + when + then (non-empty)
STEP-04: coverage beyond the nominal — at least one `error` case and one `boundary` case

output.planTest:
In-sprint Agile test plan — EPIC-1 "Claim File Comprehension" (stories BES-01: structured summary; source-referenced facts; flag/correct inaccurate summary). Certifications applied: CTFL-AT, CTAL-ATT (BDD/Gherkin, ATDD), CTAL-TA (exploratory), ISTQB AI Testing (LLM grounding/hallucination).

SCOPE & ACCEPTANCE (ATDD): three MUST stories. Acceptance is driven by the executable Gherkin scenarios below, agreed in the Three Amigos (PO / adjuster / QA) before dev starts (shift-left). Every scenario is traced to a DoD line.

TEST TYPES (test pyramid):
- Unit (automated, dev-owned): section parser, source-ref anchoring, correction persistence model, timestamping. Target: fast, deterministic.
- API/integration (automated, CI/CD): summarization endpoint contract (structured sections present), source-ref object (doc+page) on every fact, correction-capture endpoint (timestamp + file link, no auto-validation path).
- E2E (automated, gated): upload → summary render → click source ref → flag → correct → audit-log entry. Runs on the regression suite behind the feature flag.
- Exploratory (manual, session-based, CTAL-TA): 60-min charters on OCR-degraded scans, mixed-language notes, multi-document dossiers, adversarial "invented fact" hunting.
- AI-specific (ISTQB AI Testing): grounding evaluation on a gold/reference sample — 0 unsourced claims; hallucination rate below agreed threshold; non-determinism handled via metamorphic checks (same file → semantically stable summary) and a tolerance-based oracle rather than exact-string match.

DATA: pilot sample of representative claim files (native PDF, scanned/OCR, free-text notes) + a QA gold set with expected facts and their true source (doc+page) for grounding/precision measurement.

ENTRY: feature flag available; ingestion pipeline stubbed/live; gold sample signed off by business owner.
EXIT (DoD): summary <30s on pilot sample; every fact carries a clickable doc+page ref (0 unsourced on test set); corrections captured, timestamped, linked to file and feeding the quality dashboard; NO auto-validation path exists; unit+e2e green; adjuster UAT passed; audit log entry created; demoed at Sprint Review.

NON-FUNCTIONAL: performance oracle = P95 generation time < 30s on the pilot sample (report P50/P95, not a single run). Security/traceability: correction and validation events land in the audit trail (feeds EPIC-4).

METRICS (Agile quality): grounding rate (=100% target), hallucination/unsourced-claim rate, summary generation P95, correction rate per file, adjuster UAT pass rate — published to the quality dashboard for the QA retrospective and the Q3 Go/No-Go.

RISKS: LLM non-determinism (mitigate with tolerance oracle + metamorphic tests); OCR quality on scans (exploratory charter + boundary tests); silent trust of unsourced facts (hard gate: any fact lacking a source ref fails the build).

Ready-to-paste: scenarios are Gherkin/Cucumber-formatted (Given/When/Then) for direct import into Xray/Cucumber; tag suggestions @epic1 @must @ai-grounding @insprint.

gherkin.given: An adjuster has opened an incoming claim file (native PDF, scan or free-text notes) from the pilot sample
gherkin.when: They request an assisted summary of the file
gherkin.then: A structured summary is rendered within 30 seconds showing the sections loss facts, claimant, policy reference, estimated amount and key dates, and an audit log entry is created

gherkin.given: A summary has been generated for a claim file
gherkin.when: The adjuster inspects any stated fact in the summary
gherkin.then: Each fact displays a clickable source reference (document + page) that opens the exact original evidence, with zero unsourced facts in the summary

gherkin.given: A generated summary contains a fact the adjuster judges inaccurate
gherkin.when: They flag the fact and submit a correction
gherkin.then: The correction is captured, timestamped and linked to the claim file, it feeds the quality dashboard, and no automatic re-validation of the summary occurs

gherkin.given: An unreadable or corrupted document that yields no extractable text is submitted
gherkin.then: The assistant returns an explicit 'unable to extract content' message instead of fabricating facts, produces no unsourced statements, and logs the failed attempt

gherkin.given: The generation model proposes a candidate fact for which no supporting source (document + page) can be anchored
gherkin.when: The summary is assembled and validated by the grounding check
gherkin.then: The unsourced fact is withheld from the rendered summary and the case is flagged in the grounding evaluation report, keeping the unsourced-claim rate at zero on the test set

gherkin.given: A large multi-document dossier whose summarization completes at the 30-second service-level limit
gherkin.when: The adjuster requests the summary and the P95 generation time is measured on the pilot sample
gherkin.then: The structured summary is returned within the 30-second threshold (P95 < 30s), all sections are populated, and the timing is recorded to the metrics dashboard

gherkin.given: A claim file where a mandatory section (e.g. estimated amount) has no supporting evidence in the source documents
gherkin.then: The section is shown as 'not found / insufficient information' rather than an invented value, and no source reference is fabricated for it
